behavior: Actively forages through shrubs, low branches, and ground cover for insects and spiders; frequently holds its tail cocked and delivers a rapid, bubbly song from a perch. Nests in sheltered cavities, including natural holes and nest boxes.
field_marks: Very small, compact, plain gray-brown wren with a flat-headed profile.
Thin, slightly curved bill; upper mandible dark, lower mandible often paler.
Faint pale eyebrow and pale grayish throat; no strong facial pattern.
Fine dark barring across the warmer brown wings and tail.
Short wings and a relatively long, narrow tail, often cocked upward.
habitat: Open woodland, forest edge, scrub, thickets, and brushy clearings with trees or shrubs; also readily uses human-made settings that provide cover and nest cavities. Usually favors structurally varied vegetation rather than dense, closed canopy.
